content:
First, note that if $\deg f=d\ge1$ then
\[
\deg\bigl(f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)\bigr)=d,
\qquad
\deg\!\Bigl(\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}\Bigr)=2d-1.
\]
Equating degrees gives $d=1$.  Hence write
\[
f(x)=ax+b,\quad a\neq0.
\]
Then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)
=a(x-1)+b+ax+b+a(x+1)+b
=3ax+3b,
\]
while
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{(ax+b)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{a^2x^2+2abx+b^2}{2013x}
=\frac{a^2x+2ab+\tfrac{b^2}{x}}{2013}.
\]
The identity
\[
3ax+3b=\frac{a^2x+2ab+\frac{b^2}{x}}{2013}
\quad(\forall x\ne0)
\]
cleared of denominators becomes
\[
6039ax^2+6039bx
=a^2x^2+2abx+b^2,
\]
so
\[
6039a=a^2,\quad6039b=2ab,\quad0=b^2.
\]
Since $a\neq0$, $a=6039$, and then $b=0$.  Thus
\[
f(x)=6039x,
\]
and in particular $f(1)=6039$.  There is no other nonconstant solution, so the requested sum is
\[
\boxed{6039}.
\]
